title: Homeowner Furnace Failure Winter Spike
thesis: Sharp seasonal spikes in furnace/heating repair searches occur during temperature drops, signaling consumer cash outflow stress and urgent home maintenance demand. Industrial/HVAC contractors and home services benefit from repair demand spike; signals consumer willingness to spend on essential maintenance.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'furnace repair' and 'heating system maintenance'
signal_logic: If weekly Google Trends volume for 'furnace repair' rises >40% above 12-week moving average AND temperature drops below 40°F in major metro areas
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Google Trends volume for 'furnace repair' rises >40% above 12-week moving average AND temperature drops below 40°F in major metro areas Exit: After 14 calendar days or when search volume returns to baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'furnace repair' and 'heating system maintenance' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Winter heating season (Nov–Mar) reliably produces furnace search spikes tied to temperature drops; fires multiple times per season.

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'furnace repair' and 'heating system maintenance'

schema.behavior: Sharp seasonal spikes in furnace/heating repair searches occur during temperature drops, signaling consumer cash outflow stress and urgent home maintenance demand.

market_impact.rationale: Industrial/HVAC contractors and home services benefit from repair demand spike; signals consumer willingness to spend on essential maintenance.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Google Trends volume for 'furnace repair' rises >40% above 12-week moving average AND temperature drops below 40°F in major metro areas
trade_logic.exit: After 14 calendar days or when search volume returns to baseline

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Winter heating season (Nov–Mar) reliably produces furnace search spikes tied to temperature drops; fires multiple times per season.

title: Pet Emergency Vet Visit Spike
thesis: Spikes in emergency veterinary care searches indicate pet health crises (food contamination, seasonal illness), driving urgent consumer spending and signaling underlying economic stress. Veterinary and animal health services see demand surge; signals consumer financial resilience for premium health spending.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'emergency vet near me' and 'pet urgent care'
signal_logic: If weekly Google Trends volume for 'emergency vet' rises >50% above 8-week moving average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Google Trends volume for 'emergency vet' rises >50% above 8-week moving average Exit: After 10 calendar days or when volume returns to baseline + 10%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'emergency vet near me' and 'pet urgent care' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Pet health emergencies occur sporadically but reliably; seasonal patterns (hot weather, dietary shifts) ensure multiple triggers per quarter.

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'emergency vet near me' and 'pet urgent care'

schema.behavior: Spikes in emergency veterinary care searches indicate pet health crises (food contamination, seasonal illness), driving urgent consumer spending and signaling underlying economic stress.

market_impact.rationale: Veterinary and animal health services see demand surge; signals consumer financial resilience for premium health spending.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Google Trends volume for 'emergency vet' rises >50% above 8-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: After 10 calendar days or when volume returns to baseline + 10%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Pet health emergencies occur sporadically but reliably; seasonal patterns (hot weather, dietary shifts) ensure multiple triggers per quarter.

title: Appliance Shortage Supply Chain Stress
thesis: Spikes in appliance availability searches signal supply chain bottlenecks and producer input cost shocks, suggesting manufacturing and logistics stress. Supply constraints indicate upstream cost inflation and margin compression for appliance manufacturers and retailers.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'appliance shortage' and 'where to buy washing machine'
signal_logic: If weekly Google Trends volume for 'appliance shortage' rises >35% above 12-week moving average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Google Trends volume for 'appliance shortage' rises >35% above 12-week moving average Exit: After 15 trading days or when volume drops below entry baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'appliance shortage' and 'where to buy washing machine' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Supply chain disruptions occur regularly; seasonal demand surges (spring/summer home repair) create predictable shortage cycles.

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'appliance shortage' and 'where to buy washing machine'

schema.behavior: Spikes in appliance availability searches signal supply chain bottlenecks and producer input cost shocks, suggesting manufacturing and logistics stress.

market_impact.rationale: Supply constraints indicate upstream cost inflation and margin compression for appliance manufacturers and retailers.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Google Trends volume for 'appliance shortage' rises >35% above 12-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: After 15 trading days or when volume drops below entry baseline

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Supply chain disruptions occur regularly; seasonal demand surges (spring/summer home repair) create predictable shortage cycles.

title: Airline Seat Upgrade Price Surge
thesis: Rising seat upgrade and ancillary fees indicate strong airline pricing power and full cabins, signaling elevated leisure/business travel demand. Strong airline ancillary revenue signals robust discretionary spending and high travel demand; consumer sentiment on travel is bullish.
data_sources: BTS average ancillary revenue per passenger (weekly via HTML table scrape from airline earnings data proxies)
signal_logic: If weekly average ancillary revenue per passenger rises >8% above 8-week moving average AND load factor >82%
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly average ancillary revenue per passenger rises >8% above 8-week moving average AND load factor >82% Exit: After 12 trading days or when ancillary revenue retreats below entry level
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use BTS average ancillary revenue per passenger (weekly via HTML table scrape from airline earnings data proxies) via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal travel peaks (spring break, summer, holidays) reliably push ancillary revenue spikes; high-frequency data ensures multiple triggers per quarter.

data.source: BTS average ancillary revenue per passenger (weekly via HTML table scrape from airline earnings data proxies)

schema.behavior: Rising seat upgrade and ancillary fees indicate strong airline pricing power and full cabins, signaling elevated leisure/business travel demand.

market_impact.rationale: Strong airline ancillary revenue signals robust discretionary spending and high travel demand; consumer sentiment on travel is bullish.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly average ancillary revenue per passenger rises >8% above 8-week moving average AND load factor >82%
trade_logic.exit: After 12 trading days or when ancillary revenue retreats below entry level

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal travel peaks (spring break, summer, holidays) reliably push ancillary revenue spikes; high-frequency data ensures multiple triggers per quarter.

title: Freight Rate Volatility Spike
thesis: Sudden spikes in spot freight rates indicate supply-demand imbalance in trucking/shipping, signaling upstream input cost inflation and logistics stress. Rising freight costs compress margins for manufacturers, retailers, and logistics firms; signals inflation pressure on supply chains.
data_sources: Cass Freight Index or FreightWaves spot rate index (weekly via HTML scrape or RSS feed)
signal_logic: If weekly spot freight rate rises >12% above 6-week moving average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly spot freight rate rises >12% above 6-week moving average Exit: After 14 trading days or when rate retreats 8% below entry spike peak
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Cass Freight Index or FreightWaves spot rate index (weekly via HTML scrape or RSS feed) via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Freight rates are volatile and driven by seasonal demand, weather, and capacity; multiple spikes per quarter are normal.

data.source: Cass Freight Index or FreightWaves spot rate index (weekly via HTML scrape or RSS feed)

schema.behavior: Sudden spikes in spot freight rates indicate supply-demand imbalance in trucking/shipping, signaling upstream input cost inflation and logistics stress.

market_impact.rationale: Rising freight costs compress margins for manufacturers, retailers, and logistics firms; signals inflation pressure on supply chains.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly spot freight rate rises >12% above 6-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: After 14 trading days or when rate retreats 8% below entry spike peak

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Freight rates are volatile and driven by seasonal demand, weather, and capacity; multiple spikes per quarter are normal.

title: Job Listing Collapse Signal
thesis: Sharp declines in job listings signal employer caution and weakening hiring demand, foreshadowing consumer spending pressure. Falling hiring signals reduced wage growth and employment security; consumer discretionary spending contracts as job anxiety rises.
data_sources: Indeed or LinkedIn job posting volume (weekly via RSS feed or HTML table scrape of publicly available aggregate counts)
signal_logic: If weekly job listing count drops >15% below 8-week moving average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly job listing count drops >15% below 8-week moving average Exit: After 18 trading days or when listings rebound 10% above entry level
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Indeed or LinkedIn job posting volume (weekly via RSS feed or HTML table scrape of publicly available aggregate counts) via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor market sentiment shifts drive hiring cycles; seasonal slowdowns (post-holiday, summer lull) produce regular dips in job postings.

data.source: Indeed or LinkedIn job posting volume (weekly via RSS feed or HTML table scrape of publicly available aggregate counts)

schema.behavior: Sharp declines in job listings signal employer caution and weakening hiring demand, foreshadowing consumer spending pressure.

market_impact.rationale: Falling hiring signals reduced wage growth and employment security; consumer discretionary spending contracts as job anxiety rises.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly job listing count drops >15% below 8-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: After 18 trading days or when listings rebound 10% above entry level

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor market sentiment shifts drive hiring cycles; seasonal slowdowns (post-holiday, summer lull) produce regular dips in job postings.

title: Credit Card Delinquency Trend Reversal
thesis: Rising credit card delinquency rates signal consumer financial distress and reduced creditworthiness, foreshadowing discretionary spending collapse. Delinquency spikes indicate consumer balance sheet stress; retail and leisure spending deteriorates as households reduce discretionary outlays.
data_sources: Federal Reserve weekly delinquency rate data (30+ days late) via FRED series TERMCBCCALLNS
signal_logic: If weekly delinquency rate rises >15 basis points above 12-week moving average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly delinquency rate rises >15 basis points above 12-week moving average Exit: After 20 trading days or when delinquency rate declines below entry level
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Federal Reserve weekly delinquency rate data (30+ days late) via FRED series TERMCBCCALLNS via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Credit cycle peaks occur regularly; economic stress events and seasonal payment cycles create predictable delinquency spikes.

data.source: Federal Reserve weekly delinquency rate data (30+ days late) via FRED series TERMCBCCALLNS

schema.behavior: Rising credit card delinquency rates signal consumer financial distress and reduced creditworthiness, foreshadowing discretionary spending collapse.

market_impact.rationale: Delinquency spikes indicate consumer balance sheet stress; retail and leisure spending deteriorates as households reduce discretionary outlays.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly delinquency rate rises >15 basis points above 12-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: After 20 trading days or when delinquency rate declines below entry level

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Credit cycle peaks occur regularly; economic stress events and seasonal payment cycles create predictable delinquency spikes.

title: Electricity Demand Collapse During Mild Weather
thesis: Sharp electricity demand collapse during unseasonably mild weather signals reduced utility revenue and weak industrial activity. Declining electricity demand reduces utility earnings and signals softer industrial production and economic activity.
data_sources: EIA total U.S. electricity demand (daily via eia_electricity adapter) combined with Open-Meteo temperature data
signal_logic: If daily electricity demand drops >8% below 30-day moving average AND average temperature is >5°F above normal for region
entry_exit: Entry: If daily electricity demand drops >8% below 30-day moving average AND average temperature is >5°F above normal for region Exit: After 8 trading days or when demand rebounds above 30-day average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use EIA total U.S. electricity demand (daily via eia_electricity adapter) combined with Open-Meteo temperature data via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weather volatility is constant; mild spells during heating/cooling seasons produce regular demand dips.

schema.idea: Electricity Demand Collapse During Mild Weather

data.source: EIA total U.S. electricity demand (daily via eia_electricity adapter) combined with Open-Meteo temperature data

schema.behavior: Sharp electricity demand collapse during unseasonably mild weather signals reduced utility revenue and weak industrial activity.

market_impact.rationale: Declining electricity demand reduces utility earnings and signals softer industrial production and economic activity.

trade_logic.entry: If daily electricity demand drops >8% below 30-day moving average AND average temperature is >5°F above normal for region
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days or when demand rebounds above 30-day average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weather volatility is constant; mild spells during heating/cooling seasons produce regular demand dips.

title: Ev Charger Network Density Jump
thesis: Rapid expansion in public EV charger installations signals infrastructure investment and regional EV adoption acceleration, boosting tech and utilities stocks. EV charger expansion drives software/IoT infrastructure demand and signals strong regional clean energy investment; bullish for tech providers.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap EV charger count by state/region (weekly scrape of public API data)
signal_logic: If weekly EV charger count rises >20% above 12-week moving average in a region with >500 existing chargers
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly EV charger count rises >20% above 12-week moving average in a region with >500 existing chargers Exit: After 16 trading days or when monthly addition rate returns to baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap EV charger count by state/region (weekly scrape of public API data) via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Government EV subsidies and corporate commitments drive quarterly charger deployment waves; infrastructure grants create regular spike cycles.

data.source: OpenChargeMap EV charger count by state/region (weekly scrape of public API data)

schema.behavior: Rapid expansion in public EV charger installations signals infrastructure investment and regional EV adoption acceleration, boosting tech and utilities stocks.

market_impact.rationale: EV charger expansion drives software/IoT infrastructure demand and signals strong regional clean energy investment; bullish for tech providers.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly EV charger count rises >20% above 12-week moving average in a region with >500 existing chargers
trade_logic.exit: After 16 trading days or when monthly addition rate returns to baseline

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Government EV subsidies and corporate commitments drive quarterly charger deployment waves; infrastructure grants create regular spike cycles.

title: Earthquake Swarm Activity Spike
thesis: Elevated earthquake frequency in seismically active regions (CA, WA, OK) signals geological stress and triggers property insurance and structural repair demand surges. Earthquake swarms drive construction, engineering services, and property repair demand; signals short-term industrial/materials activity boost.
data_sources: USGS earthquake activity feed (daily: count of magnitude 3.0+ earthquakes per week by region)
signal_logic: If weekly 3.0+ magnitude earthquake count in CA/WA/OK exceeds 8-week moving average by >150%
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly 3.0+ magnitude earthquake count in CA/WA/OK exceeds 8-week moving average by >150% Exit: After 20 trading days or when weekly count returns to baseline moving average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake activity feed (daily: count of magnitude 3.0+ earthquakes per week by region) via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic activity clusters occur regularly in fault zones; swarms lasting 2–4 weeks are common and trigger repair demand within days.

data.source: USGS earthquake activity feed (daily: count of magnitude 3.0+ earthquakes per week by region)

schema.behavior: Elevated earthquake frequency in seismically active regions (CA, WA, OK) signals geological stress and triggers property insurance and structural repair demand surges.

market_impact.rationale: Earthquake swarms drive construction, engineering services, and property repair demand; signals short-term industrial/materials activity boost.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly 3.0+ magnitude earthquake count in CA/WA/OK exceeds 8-week moving average by >150%
trade_logic.exit: After 20 trading days or when weekly count returns to baseline moving average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic activity clusters occur regularly in fault zones; swarms lasting 2–4 weeks are common and trigger repair demand within days.
